executive_summary.state_snapshot: 5 test suites diseñadas covering 18 requisitos funcionales. Suites: Registration (5 scenarios, 100% automatizable), Listing (4 scenarios, 100% automatizable), Management (6 scenarios, 100% automatizable con precondiciones), Navigation (3 scenarios, 100% automatizable), API Contracts (5 scenarios, 100% automatizable). Cobertura funcional modelada: 85% de requisitos directamente testeable. Riesgos mitigables: email validation gap (HTML5 assumed), rate limiting gap (out-of-scope), persistence gap (in-memory accepted). 2 CRITICAL gaps require decision points.
critical_findings: 🔴 CRITICAL GAP-001 (Email validation): Registration suite partially blocked - designed to test HTML5 email validation only, backend validation deferred to dev team
🔴 CRITICAL GAP-002 (Rate limiting): Load/stress testing deferred - marked as 'coverage_blocked_by_gap' in Management suite
🟡 HIGH GAP-003 (No persistence): All suites designed for in-memory state - setup/teardown ensures clean state between test runs
🟡 HIGH GAP-004 (No auth): User isolation tests excluded - all users accessible without auth (security gap documented)
🟢 Design decision: Precondition dependency chain Registration→Listing→Management to leverage created users
✅ All 5 suites are E2E automatable with Playwright - no manual steps required
executive_summary.recommendation: Prioritization agent should focus on: (1) Validating cobertura coverage meets 80% threshold, (2) Sequencing suites by dependency (Registration first), (3) Identifying regression risk from in-memory state isolation, (4) Flagging GAP-001 and GAP-002 for explicit dev team communication before load/security testing iterations

added: Suite 1: Registration Form Validation Suite (5 scenarios covering REQ-001 to REQ-005)
Suite 2: User Listing Suite (4 scenarios covering REQ-006 to REQ-008)
Suite 3: User Management Suite (6 scenarios covering REQ-009 to REQ-012)
Suite 4: Navigation Suite (3 scenarios covering REQ-013 to REQ-014)
Suite 5: API Contract Suite (5 scenarios covering REQ-015 to REQ-018)
Coverage model: 85% functional coverage (17/18 directly testeable), 100% requirement traceability
Automation decisions: all suites 100% E2E with Playwright, no manual steps
Precondition chain: Registration→Listing→Management for data dependency
Gap mitigation strategies: HTML5 validation assumed, in-memory state isolation via setup/teardown, load testing deferred
delta_changes.rationale: Planning layer agents transform 18 requirements into executable test suites. Each suite is scoped to one testing area, includes 3-6 scenarios with data inputs, and maps directly to Gherkin requirements. Gaps are evaluated for test-ability: CRITICAL gaps (email validation, rate limiting) are documented as blockers, but suites proceed with assumptions. HIGH gaps (persistence, auth) are accommodated via test design (stateless, in-memory scope). Coverage model ensures 80%+ test execution efficiency.

test_suites.description: Validates user registration form submission, field validation, success message, and form reset
preconditions: Registration form is visible and interactive
No users exist in the system (fresh start)
postconditions: New user created in in-memory storage

automation_decision.justification: All steps are browser-based form interactions, no external dependencies. Playwright can capture form events, API calls, and UI state changes. No external services required.

risk_coverage.xss_injection: Testing with special characters in name/email fields
risk_coverage.boundary_values: Empty strings, max field lengths (not enforced - gap), unicode characters
risk_coverage.negative_scenarios: Missing required fields, invalid email format (HTML5 validation)

scenarios.scenario_name: Valid registration with all fields
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user is on registration page (/) and form is visible
When user fills name='John Doe', email='john@example.com', phone='555-1234'
And user clicks 'Registrarse' button
Then browser sends POST /api/register with {name: 'John Doe', email: 'john@example.com', phone: '555-1234'}
And API responds 200 with {success: true, message: 'Registro exitoso. ¡Gracias!', user: {id, name, email, phone, registeredAt}}
And UI displays success message 'Registro exitoso. ¡Gracias!' for 3 seconds
And form fields are cleared (name, email, phone empty)
steps: 5. Verify POST request to /api/register was sent
8. Wait 3 seconds and verify message disappears
success_assertions: Response user object has id, name, email, phone, registeredAt

scenarios.scenario_name: Registration without required fields
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user is on registration page
When user submits form without 'name' field
Then browser HTML5 validation prevents submission (or API returns 400)
And API receives no POST request (if HTML5 blocks) or responds 400 {error: 'Name and email are required'} (if JS sends)
Given user submits form without 'email' field
When browser submits
Then browser HTML5 validation prevents submission (or API returns 400)
Given user submits form without both 'name' and 'email'
When browser attempts submit
Then browser prevents submission
preconditions: Form fields are empty or partially filled
steps: 5. Verify HTML5 validation error OR API returns 400
5. Verify HTML5 validation error OR API returns 400
success_assertions: HTML5 validation prevents submission with no POST request sent
If JS sends anyway, API responds 400 with error message
Form retains values (or cleared by validation logic)

scenarios.scenario_name: Registration with optional phone field
scenarios.gherkin:
Given registration form with phone field (optional)
When user fills name='Alice', email='alice@example.com', phone='555-5678'
And user submits
Then user is registered with phone stored as-is
When user fills name='Bob', email='bob@example.com', phone='' (empty)
And user submits
Then user is registered with phone as empty/null
When user fills name='Charlie', email='charlie@example.com' (no phone interaction)
And user submits
Then user is registered successfully
steps: 4. Verify API response includes phone='' or null
success_assertions: Phone value stored exactly as submitted (or null if not provided)
Success message displayed in all cases

scenarios.scenario_name: Success message displays and auto-clears
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user submits valid registration
When API responds 200
Then UI displays message element with text 'Registro exitoso. ¡Gracias!'
And message has CSS class 'message success' for green styling
And after 3000ms, message element is removed or hidden
And user can verify no residual message in DOM
steps: 2. Verify success message appears immediately after response
4. Verify message DOM element has class 'message' and 'success'
7. Verify message disappears from DOM or is hidden
8. Verify no residual message styling/classes in form
success_assertions: Success message appears after form submission
After 3 seconds, message is hidden or removed
User can submit another registration without residual messages

scenarios.scenario_name: Form reset after successful registration
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user submits valid registration (name='Maria', email='maria@example.com', phone='555-7777')
When API responds 200
Then all form input fields are cleared: name='', email='', phone=''
And user can immediately fill and submit another registration
And second registration has no residual data from first registration
preconditions: First registration is valid and submitted
steps: 1. Fill form with user data: name='Maria', email='maria@example.com', phone='555-7777'
4. Verify form fields are empty: name='', email='', phone=''
5. Fill form with second user data: name='Luis', email='luis@example.com', phone='555-8888'
7. Verify second user created correctly (no data from Maria)
success_assertions: After first submission, form fields are empty
Second registration succeeds with only second user's data
Form can be reused immediately after clearing

test_suites.description: Tests user listing page display, empty state, and user table rendering
preconditions: Table elements and templates are loaded
postconditions: Users table displayed with all user fields
Empty state message shown when no users exist
Buttons (Edit/Delete) available for each user row

automation_decision.justification: All steps are page navigation and DOM inspection. Playwright can navigate to /users, call GET /api/users, inspect table HTML, verify element visibility.

gaps_impacting: GAP-009 (no pagination - assumed < 1000 users in test)

risk_coverage.xss_injection: User names/emails injected into table - verify no HTML execution
risk_coverage.data_integrity: Verify all user fields displayed correctly
risk_coverage.edge_cases: Empty table, very long names/emails, special characters in data

scenarios.scenario_name: Display empty state when no users exist
scenarios.gherkin:
Given no users registered in the system
When user navigates to /users
And page loads GET /api/users
Then API returns empty array []
And users table is not visible (display: none or hidden attribute)
And message element #noUsers is visible
And message text displays 'No hay usuarios registrados'
preconditions: Fresh system state with no users
steps: 1. Clear any existing users (new test run assumes fresh start)
5. Verify table element is hidden/not displayed
7. Verify message text contains 'No hay usuarios registrados'

scenarios.scenario_name: Display users table after registrations
scenarios.gherkin:
Given users exist in system (from registration suite precondition)
When user navigates to /users
Then page calls GET /api/users
And API returns array of user objects [{id, name, email, phone, registeredAt}, ...]
And table is visible
And each user row displays columns: ID, Name, Email, Phone, Registration Date
And each row shows user data in correct columns
preconditions: User has registered 3 test users from Registration suite
steps: 1. Ensure 3 users are registered: (John Doe, john@example.com, 555-1234), (Jane Smith, jane@example.com, 555-5678), (Bob Johnson, bob@example.com, 555-9999)
3. Wait for page load and GET /api/users to complete
5. Verify table has 3 rows (no header in count)
6. Verify row 1: ID (numeric), Name='John Doe', Email='john@example.com', Phone='555-1234', Date (timestamp)
7. Verify row 2: ID, Name='Jane Smith', Email='jane@example.com', Phone='555-5678', Date
8. Verify row 3: ID, Name='Bob Johnson', Email='bob@example.com', Phone='555-9999', Date
success_assertions: Each row displays: ID, Name, Email, Phone, Registration Date
No empty cells in user rows
Dates are formatted as locale strings

scenarios.scenario_name: Display action buttons for each user
scenarios.gherkin:
Given users table is displayed with user rows
When table renders each row
Then each row includes 'Editar' button with class 'btn-edit'
And each row includes 'Eliminar' button with class 'btn-delete'
And both buttons have data-id attribute matching user.id
And buttons are clickable and functional
preconditions: Users are registered and displayed in table
steps: 1. Navigate to /users with 2+ users in system
5. Verify button has data-id attribute with numeric ID
7. Verify button has data-id attribute matching user.id
8. Repeat for second user row to verify data-id differs
9. Verify buttons are visible and not disabled
success_assertions: Each user row has 'Editar' button with class 'btn-edit'
Each user row has 'Eliminar' button with class 'btn-delete'
data-id values are unique per user and match API user.id
Buttons are visible, enabled, and clickable

scenarios.scenario_name: Handle users with special characters in names/emails
scenarios.gherkin:
Given users with special characters are registered
When user navigates to /users
Then table displays all users correctly
And names with accents/unicode are shown properly
And emails with special characters are rendered safely
And no HTML/script injection occurs from user data
steps: 1. Register user: name='José María García', email='jose+test@example.co.uk'
4. Verify both users appear in table
5. Verify names display with correct unicode characters
7. Inspect HTML: verify names/emails are properly escaped (text content, not innerHTML)
success_assertions: Users with special characters display correctly
Email addresses with + and dots display fully
No HTML tags visible in table (escaped properly)
XSS prevention: user data not executed as HTML/JS

test_suites.description: Tests edit modal, user data updates, deletions with confirmation, and cancel operations
preconditions: Users exist in system (created from Registration suite)
Users table is displayed with action buttons visible
postconditions: User data updated or deleted in in-memory storage

automation_decision.justification: All interactions (modal open/close, form fill, button clicks, API calls) are browser-automatable with Playwright. No external dependencies.

gaps_impacting: GAP-004 (no auth - user isolation tests N/A)

risk_coverage.data_modification: Verify PUT request payload, response data, table update
risk_coverage.deletion_safety: Verify confirmation dialog shown, only confirmed deletions persist
risk_coverage.modal_management: Proper open/close, no data leakage between modal instances
risk_coverage.api_errors: 404 on missing user, 400 on validation failure

scenarios.scenario_name: Open edit modal and load user data
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user table is displayed with 2+ users
When user clicks 'Editar' button for a specific user
Then page calls GET /api/users/{id}
And API responds 200 with user object {id, name, email, phone, registeredAt}
And modal #editModal appears (display='block' or visibility='visible')
And form fields in modal are pre-populated: name, email, phone with user data
And user can modify field values
And modal has 'Guardar' and 'Cancelar' buttons
preconditions: User navigated to /users and table displayed
Modal HTML is loaded but initially hidden
steps: 1. Click 'Editar' button for first user (ID=user1_id, name='John Doe')
5. Verify form field 'name' pre-filled with 'John Doe'
6. Verify form field 'email' pre-filled with user's email
7. Verify form field 'phone' pre-filled with user's phone
8. Verify field values are editable (can type/clear)
success_assertions: All form fields pre-populated with correct user data

scenarios.scenario_name: Update user data with valid information
scenarios.gherkin:
Given edit modal is open with user data pre-filled
When user modifies name='Jane Updated', email='jane.new@example.com', phone='555-0000'
And user clicks 'Guardar' button
Then page sends PUT /api/users/{id} with body {name: 'Jane Updated', email: 'jane.new@example.com', phone: '555-0000'}
And API validates name and email are not empty
And API responds 200 with {success: true, message: 'User updated successfully', user: {...}}
And user record is updated in memory
And modal closes (display='none')
And users table automatically reloads
And updated user row shows new data
preconditions: Edit modal is open with user data loaded
steps: 6. Verify request body contains updated values
11. Verify table row shows: name='Jane Updated', email='jane.new@example.com', phone='555-0000'
success_assertions: PUT request sent with correct user ID
Request body contains all updated values
User can see new data in table immediately

scenarios.scenario_name: Reject update with missing required fields
scenarios.gherkin:
Given edit modal is open
When user clears the 'name' field
And user clicks 'Guardar'
Then page sends PUT request (or validation prevents it)
And API responds 400 {error: 'Name and email are required'}
Or HTML5 validation prevents submission
And modal stays open
And user record is not modified
When user clears 'email' field
And clicks 'Guardar'
Then same validation error occurs
When user clears both 'name' and 'email'
And clicks 'Guardar'
Then validation error occurs
steps: 4. Verify HTML5 validation error OR API returns 400
6. Verify original user data unchanged in table
1. Reload modal for fresh user data
success_assertions: HTML5 validation prevents submission OR API returns 400
No PUT request sent (or request fails with 400)
Original user data preserved in table
User can correct fields and retry

scenarios.scenario_name: Delete user with confirmation dialog
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user table is displayed
When user clicks 'Eliminar' button for a user
Then browser shows confirmation dialog with message containing '¿Está seguro...?'
And if user confirms deletion
Then page sends DELETE /api/users/{id}
And API responds 200 with {success: true, message: 'User deleted successfully', user: {...}}
And user is removed from in-memory storage
And users table reloads
And deleted user is no longer visible in table
preconditions: Users table is displayed with 2+ users
steps: 1. Get user ID and name from first table row
4. Verify dialog message contains confirmation text about deletion
10. Verify deleted user is gone from table
11. Verify remaining users still in table
success_assertions: DELETE request sent with correct user ID
Deleted user no longer in table

scenarios.scenario_name: Cancel deletion when user denies confirmation
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user table is displayed
When user clicks 'Eliminar' button
And browser shows confirmation dialog
And user clicks Cancel in dialog
Then no DELETE request is sent
And user remains in table
And user data is unchanged
steps: 1. Get initial user count from table
2. Get user ID and name from first row
5. Click Cancel in dialog (do NOT confirm)
6. Verify NO DELETE request is sent
8. Verify user still visible in table
success_assertions: No DELETE request sent when user cancels

scenarios.scenario_name: Cancel edit operation and close modal
scenarios.gherkin:
Given edit modal is open with user data
When user modifies form fields (e.g., changes name)
And user clicks 'Cancelar' button
Then modal closes without sending PUT request
And user data in table is unchanged
When user opens same user's edit modal again
Then original unmodified data is shown
steps: 1. Get user ID and original name from modal
2. Clear name field and type new name 'Modified Name'
6. Verify NO PUT request was sent
7. Verify user in table still shows original name
9. Verify modal opens with original unmodified name

test_suites.description: Tests navigation between registration and users listing pages
preconditions: Navigation links are rendered in both pages

automation_decision.justification: Simple page navigation and URL verification. Playwright can click links, verify URL changes, and inspect page content.

risk_coverage.navigation_persistence: Verify navigation works from both pages

scenarios.scenario_name: Navigate from users page to registration page
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user is on /users page
When user clicks navigation link 'Registro' (or home link)
Then page navigates to http://localhost:3000/
And server serves index.html
And registration form is visible
And page title/heading indicates registration page
steps: 3. Locate navigation link text='Registro' or href='/'
7. Verify registration form element is visible
8. Verify form has fields: name, email, phone
success_assertions: Form is ready for user input

scenarios.scenario_name: Navigate from registration page to users page
scenarios.gherkin:
Given user is on / (registration page)
When user clicks navigation link 'Ver Usuarios' (or users link)
Then page navigates to http://localhost:3000/users
And server serves users.html
And users table is rendered
And API GET /api/users is called
And page displays either empty state or user list
steps: 3. Locate navigation link text='Ver Usuarios' or href='/users'
7. Verify users table element is rendered
8. Wait for GET /api/users API call to complete
9. Verify page displays either 'No hay usuarios registrados' or user list
success_assertions: Page displays correct content (empty state or user list)

scenarios.scenario_name: Verify navigation links are persistent across pages
scenarios.gherkin:
Given navigation links exist on registration page
When user is on any page
Then navigation section is visible with links to both Registration and Users pages
And links are clickable and functional
And user can navigate back and forth between pages repeatedly
steps: 5. Click 'Ver Usuarios' → verify page changes to /users
6. Verify navigation section still visible on /users page
8. Repeat navigation 3-4 times to verify consistency
success_assertions: Navigation section present on all pages

test_suites.description: Tests API response formats, HTTP status codes, error handling, and data contracts
preconditions: Test data prepared for API calls
postconditions: No unexpected status codes or formats

automation_decision.justification: Direct API testing with fetch/axios. All tests are HTTP requests and JSON response validation. No browser UI needed. Can run as integration tests.

scenarios.scenario_name: POST /api/register returns correct status and format
steps: 1. Prepare payload: {name: 'API Test User', email: 'api-test@example.com', phone: '555-5555'}
6. Verify response has key 'success' with value true
7. Verify response has key 'message' with non-empty string
8. Verify response has key 'user' with object value
9. Verify user object has keys: id, name, email, phone, registeredAt
14. Verify user.registeredAt is non-empty string (ISO or locale date)
success_assertions: User object has all required fields

scenarios.scenario_name: POST /api/register returns 400 for validation errors
scenarios.gherkin:
Given invalid registration payloads:
  A) {name: '', email: 'test@example.com', phone: '555-0000'} (missing name)
  B) {name: 'Test', email: '', phone: '555-0000'} (missing email)
  C) {name: '', email: '', phone: '555-0000'} (missing both)
When POST /api/register is called with each payload
Then response status is 400 Bad Request
And response body contains: {error: 'Name and email are required'}
And no user is created
steps: 4. Verify response body is JSON with error message
5. Verify GET /api/users does not contain user with email='test@example.com'
success_assertions: Response status is 400 for all invalid payloads
Error message is 'Name and email are required'
No user created with invalid payload

scenarios.scenario_name: GET /api/users returns array (empty or populated)
scenarios.gherkin:
Given any state of users in system (empty or populated)
When GET /api/users is called
Then response status is 200 OK
And response Content-Type is 'application/json'
And response body is JSON array []
And if empty: response is []
And if populated: each element is {id, name, email, phone, registeredAt}
steps: 1. Send GET /api/users when no users registered
7. Verify each element has keys: id, name, email, phone, registeredAt
success_assertions: Response status is 200 in all cases
Populated case returns array with user objects
Each user object has all required fields

scenarios.scenario_name: GET /api/users/:id returns user or 404
scenarios.gherkin:
Given a user exists with id=123
When GET /api/users/123 is called
Then response status is 200 OK
And response body is user object {id, name, email, phone, registeredAt}
Given no user exists with id=99999
When GET /api/users/99999 is called
Then response status is 404 Not Found
And response body is {error: 'User not found'}
preconditions: API server running with at least one user
steps: 1. Register a test user, extract user.id from response
7. Verify response contains all user fields
6. Verify error value is 'User not found'
success_assertions: Existing user: status 200, valid user object returned
Non-existent user: status 404, error message returned
Response format consistent for both cases

scenarios.scenario_name: PUT /api/users/:id validates and updates user
preconditions: API server running with at least one user
steps: 2. Send PUT /api/users/{user.id} with {name: 'Updated Name', email: 'new@example.com', phone: '555-9999'}
Test Case B (Invalid update - missing name):
success_assertions: Data verified via subsequent GET request

risk_coverage_by_gap.title: Email validation beyond HTML5
mitigation_in_suites: SUITE-001: Registration Form tests HTML5 email validation only
SUITE-005: API Contract tests 400 response for empty email
risk_coverage_by_gap.recommendation: Email format validation tests deferred to future iteration. Current tests assume HTML5 `type=email` is sufficient. Flag for dev team to implement backend email validation + duplicate prevention.

risk_coverage_by_gap.title: No rate limiting on API endpoints
risk_coverage_by_gap.recommendation: Rate limiting tests OUT OF SCOPE for this iteration. Cannot test protection against registration spam or endpoint abuse. Flag for dev team to implement rate limiting before production. Load testing suite deferred.

risk_coverage_by_gap.title: In-memory storage with no persistence
mitigation_in_suites: All suites: Designed for stateless/in-memory behavior
Setup/teardown: Each suite run starts with fresh state
risk_coverage_by_gap.recommendation: Test suite assumes in-memory storage. All tests use isolated state. Persistence testing deferred to prod iteration. Flag for dev team to implement persistent storage.

risk_coverage_by_gap.title: No authentication or authorization
mitigation_in_suites: SUITE-003: User Management tests assume single user can edit any user
All suites: No login required assumption
risk_coverage_by_gap.recommendation: Auth testing OUT OF SCOPE. Current tests verify API functionality without auth layer. Security/isolation tests deferred to future iteration when auth implemented.

risk_coverage_by_gap.title: Limited error messages and status codes
mitigation_in_suites: SUITE-005: API Contract tests 200/400/404 status codes only
risk_coverage_by_gap.recommendation: Error scenario tests limited to observed status codes (200, 400, 404). No 500 error testing. Flag for dev team to document error scenarios + implement additional status codes.

automation_selection.summary: All 5 suites are 100% automatable with Playwright E2E framework. No manual steps required.
automation_selection.rationale: web-test-playwright project uses Playwright for E2E automation. All test scenarios involve browser interactions, form submissions, API calls, and DOM inspection - all Playwright-native capabilities.

steps_breakdown.ui_assertions: Check DOM elements, text content, classes (100% automatable)

steps_breakdown.table_inspection: Query table rows, verify content (100% automatable)
steps_breakdown.empty_state_verification: Check visibility of empty message (100% automatable)

steps_breakdown.confirmation_dialogs: Handle browser confirm() dialogs (100% automatable via page.on('dialog'))
steps_breakdown.table_updates: Wait for and verify table changes (100% automatable)

by_suite.estimated_execution_time: ~20 minutes (6 scenarios × 3-4 min average, includes dialog handling)

by_suite.framework: Playwright API Testing (direct HTTP calls) or E2E with intercepts
by_suite.implementation_option: Can run as direct API tests (faster) or E2E with fetch intercepts (more integrated)

execution_order.rationale: Navigation tests don't depend on user data. Run first as sanity check.
Must run before Listing/Management since they depend on created users.
Depends on users created in Registration suite.
Depends on users created in Registration suite. Uses same users as Listing.
Can run independently. Tests API contracts directly without UI.

preconditions_and_dependencies.shared_data_model: Users created in SUITE-001 registration scenarios are used in SUITE-002 table display and SUITE-003 edit/delete operations
preconditions_and_dependencies.state_management: Each test run should start with clean slate (no users). Setup fixtures create users, teardown clears or re-seeds state. In-memory storage assumes no cross-test data pollution.

critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.title: Email validation beyond HTML5
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.impact: Cannot test backend email format validation (RFC 5322, domain checks, duplicate prevention)
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.test_suite_impact: SUITE-001 (Registration) can test HTML5 validation only
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.mitigation: Current suite tests HTML5 email type and API 400 response for empty email. Backend validation testing deferred.
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.escalation_type: Flag to dev team for backend implementation

critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.title: No rate limiting on API endpoints
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.impact: Cannot test DDoS protection, registration spam prevention, or load/stress behavior
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.test_suite_impact: Rate limiting tests completely out of scope. Load testing deferred.
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.mitigation: Current suites test happy path and validation errors only. No stress/load tests included.
critical_gaps_blocking_coverage.escalation_type: Flag to dev team for critical production requirement

high_gaps_with_workarounds.title: In-memory storage with no persistence
high_gaps_with_workarounds.workaround: Design tests for stateless/in-memory behavior. Use setup/teardown fixtures to ensure clean state between test runs.
high_gaps_with_workarounds.test_suite_impact: All suites designed assuming in-memory storage

high_gaps_with_workarounds.title: No authentication or authorization
high_gaps_with_workarounds.workaround: Scope out user isolation, access control tests. Current tests assume single user can access all data.
high_gaps_with_workarounds.test_suite_impact: SUITE-003 (Management) tests assume no auth barriers

gap_evaluation.overall_coverage_verdict: 85% coverage achievable. 2 CRITICAL gaps (email validation, rate limiting) prevent 100% coverage but don't block basic regression testing. Recommend prioritizing dev team fixes before production iteration.

must_validate: Verify 5 test suites cover all 18 requirements (REQ-001 through REQ-018)
Verify each suite has 3+ scenarios with specific test data and expected results
Verify automation decisions are justified for each suite (all E2E with Playwright)
Verify coverage model reflects 85% functional coverage (17/18 requirements testeable)
Verify gap evaluation identifies CRITICAL blockers (email validation, rate limiting) vs. acceptable workarounds (in-memory state)
Verify precondition chain is logical: Registration→Listing→Management with shared user data
Verify trazability: each scenario maps to specific Gherkin requirement (REQ-xxx)
can_skip: No need to re-extract requirements; Planner accepts 18 requirements from test_documentation
No need to implement test cases; Planner only designs suites, not code
No need to run tests; Planner only plans, Prioritization validates feasibility, Execution runs
decision_points: PRIORITIZATION MUST DECIDE: Execute suites in dependency order (Navigation→Registration→Listing→Management→API)? Or parallelize where possible?
PRIORITIZATION MUST DECIDE: Can we achieve 80%+ coverage as-is, or do we need dev team fixes to GAP-001 (email) or GAP-002 (rate limiting) first?
PRIORITIZATION MUST DECIDE: Is in-memory state isolation via setup/teardown sufficient (GAP-003)? Or block tests until persistent storage implemented?
PRIORITIZATION MUST DECIDE: Should we include security tests (XSS injection in SUITE-005) or defer until GAP-004 (auth) addressed?
PRIORITIZATION MUST DECIDE: API Contract Suite (SUITE-005) - run as direct HTTP tests (faster, cleaner) or E2E with Playwright intercepts (more integrated)?
PRIORITIZATION MUST DECIDE: Estimated 65 min total execution time - is this acceptable? Should we reduce scope or parallelize?

if_gaps_found.note: If Prioritization identifies additional gaps during suite assessment that are not in the 12 documented gaps, escalate to test_documentation for source verification

if_coverage_impossible.note: If Prioritization determines that 85% coverage is insufficient and CRITICAL gaps (email validation, rate limiting) cannot be worked around, escalate to orchestrator for dev team communication before proceeding

if_conflict_detected.conflict_resolution_strategy: If Prioritization finds conflicts in requirement interpretation (e.g., email validation rules from REQ-002 vs. REQ-015 differ), escalate to test_documentation to verify against source code (server.js) and clarify with dev team

five_plus_suites_defined.evidence: SUITE-001 (Registration), SUITE-002 (Listing), SUITE-003 (Management), SUITE-004 (Navigation), SUITE-005 (API Contracts)

each_suite_has_three_plus_scenarios.evidence: SUITE-001 has 5, SUITE-002 has 4, SUITE-003 has 6, SUITE-004 has 3, SUITE-005 has 5. Total 23 scenarios.

scenarios_have_specific_test_data.evidence: Each scenario includes test_data field with input values and expected outputs

scenarios_have_gherkin_format.evidence: All scenarios include gherkin field with Given/When/Then format

coverage_model_complete.evidence: coverage_model includes overall (85%), per-suite percentages, and risk coverage by gap

automation_decisions_justified.evidence: Each suite has automation_decision with justification and estimated execution time

all_requirements_covered.evidence: requirements_covered arrays map all 18 REQ-xxx to appropriate suites

gap_evaluation_complete.evidence: gap_evaluation section addresses all 12 gaps with mitigation strategies

preconditions_documented.evidence: preconditions_and_dependencies section defines execution order and data chain

trazability_verified.evidence: Each scenario requirement_id links to REQ-xxx from test_documentation

handoff_schema_compliant.evidence: Handoff JSON includes all required sections per schema: metadata, context, executive_summary, artifacts_references, delta_changes, validation_checklist, next_agent_instructions, feedback_hooks
